title: carica microcarpa subsp. baccata
intro: Carica microcarpa subsp. baccata, also known as the Christmas tree papaya, is a species of papaya that is native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is a type of tropical fruit-bearing shrub that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The fruit, leaves, and roots of the Christmas tree papaya have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the fruit and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as reducing pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
